description: Large binary assets the historical proof collection depends on. An outsider needs these present and sha256-matching for the FULL collection to run. The Thompson lookup-table .npz was the asset flagged as missing in the v0.11 external critique (it was excluded from the v0.0.1 public tree). Each entry says whether the asset is VENDORED in-repo or must be FETCHED/REBUILT, and how to regenerate it deterministically from pristine WRF.
generated_by: scripts/verify_reproducibility.sh (asset-pin check) -- regenerate with the rebuild_cmd below

assets.name: thompson-tables-v1
assets.rebuild_notes: Compiles a tiny Fortran harness against the UNMODIFIED pristine WRF module_mp_thompson.F, dumps the freezeH2O / qr_acr_qg / qr_acr_qs / collision-efficiency lookup tables, and packs them into the .npz. Requires gfortran + a WRF checkout. The 29 MB binary is vendored in this repo so an outsider does NOT need to rebuild it; rebuild is only for provenance regeneration. This is the asset the v0.11 critique flagged as the FULL-collection blocker (it was excluded from the v0.0.1 public push).

assets.name: rrtmg-tables-v1
assets.pin_discrepancy: fixtures/manifests/analytic-rrtmg-{sw,lw}-column-v1.yaml and rrtmg-intermediate-oracle-v1.yaml pin checksum_sha256=5cc63950c81795b02f7bf47147afc4a9d667ae00f14510f7325816fb4f3a06e2, but the on-disk asset is 0695e523a5c79025721eb3e660e379ca84d246ec791ecc24b2b6aafd5296a5f4. The asserting test (test_m5_rrtmg_tables.py) only checks the digest is 64 hex chars, NOT that it matches the manifest pin, so this stale pin does not currently fail any gate. PRE-EXISTING (not introduced by v0.13 reproducibility work); the manifest pin should be refreshed in a radiation-owned sprint.

WRF_PRISTINE_ROOT.what: An UNMODIFIED pristine WRF v4 source checkout (default: a `wrf_pristine/WRF` sibling of this repo). Override with the WRF_PRISTINE_ROOT env var.
WRF_PRISTINE_ROOT.needed_for: ONLY oracle (re)generation and Fortran-linked savepoint-parity proofs that recompile/hash the WRF source. The vendored savepoints + .npz tables let the CPU proof collection run WITHOUT it.
WRF_PRISTINE_ROOT.license_note: WRF is a separate, openly available codebase (UCAR/NCAR); it is intentionally NOT vendored here.

corpus_runs.what: Real CPU-WRF forecast output (wrfout) + AIFS forcing used by the operational/TOST equivalence proofs.
corpus_runs.location_on_dev_machine: /mnt/data/canairy_meteo/runs and /mnt/data/wrf_gpu2 (purged from most dirs; see docs/fixture-storage-policy.md)
corpus_runs.needed_for: proofs/m20/* (TOST), proofs/v0*/ multi-day operational gates, proofs/v090/*_savepoint_parity oracle dirs.
corpus_runs.outsider_status: NOT reproducible by an outsider without re-running CPU-WRF from the AIFS forcing (data/manifests/aifs_ingest_v0.json documents the source).

gpu.what: An NVIDIA GPU + CUDA-enabled JAX for the performance/speedup, multi-GPU, and 1km nested-domain proofs.
gpu.needed_for: proofs/perf/*, proofs/multigpu_dgx/*, proofs/v0120/* nested-domain gates, any speedup claim.
gpu.outsider_status: Numeric correctness is reproducible on CPU; speedup/throughput claims require GPU.
